findings.description: In the foreground of the South-facing view, there is a clearly visible pothole with irregular, jagged edges where asphalt has broken away. The hole shows exposed darker base material and has collected some debris. The depression is visible in the roadway near parked vehicles, with crumbling edges characteristic of pavement failure.

candidates.description: This location shows a narrow urban alleyway between high-rise buildings in what appears to be Panama City. The road surface is concrete/asphalt with normal wear patterns. There are parking areas, building entrances with gates, and typical urban infrastructure including drainage grates and curb cuts. No potholes are visible.

In the foreground of the South-facing view, there is a clearly visible pothole with irregular, jagged edges where asphalt has broken away. The hole shows exposed darker base material and has collected some debris. The depression is visible in the roadway near parked vehicles, with crumbling edges characteristic of pavement failure.

The road surface shows weathered asphalt with extensive surface cracking and patching throughout. There are visible cracks and uneven surfaces, but no discrete holes where asphalt material is physically missing. The pavement appears aged with some water staining but maintains structural integrity.

The road surface shows extensive cracking and general deterioration with patched areas visible throughout. The pavement has significant alligator cracking patterns and surface wear, particularly visible in the southeast and south-facing views. There is a patched utility area with a metal cover visible in the northeast view, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are present.

This location shows a narrow urban street/alley in what appears to be Panama City's financial district. The road surface is concrete with normal wear patterns. Images show building entrances, parking areas, and standard urban infrastructure including bollards, curbs, and drainage features. No potholes or significant asphalt damage visible.

The location shows an urban street area in Panama City with modern high-rise buildings, paved pedestrian areas with brick pavers, and standard road infrastructure. The 'Down' view shows the street surface with bollards (black and white posts with chains) marking a boundary. The road surface appears wet but intact with no visible potholes or significant asphalt damage.

debug_points.description: The road surface shows general wear and deterioration with rough texture, uneven patches, and some surface degradation. There are areas of discoloration and weathering visible across the pavement, particularly in the access road/parking area. No discrete holes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are visible.

The downward-facing image shows a wet street surface with minor surface wear and deterioration. The pavement has some roughness and discoloration but no discrete holes or missing asphalt. Black and white bollards with chains are visible at the curb edge, and there's a drainage channel/gutter running along the street edge.

The road surface shows weathered asphalt with extensive surface cracking and patching throughout. There are visible cracks and uneven surfaces, but no discrete holes where asphalt material is physically missing. The pavement appears aged with some water staining but maintains structural integrity.

There is a patched utility area with a metal cover visible in the northeast view, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt are present.

Black and white traffic bollards are present on the sidewalk edge, and the road has normal urban wear patterns.
